items.title: Watch: Match of the Day
items.summary: Kelly Cates presents highlights from eight Premier League games.

items.title: Ball loses WBA featherweight title after brutal knockout defeat to Figueroa
items.summary: Nick Ball lost his WBA featherweight title after a knockout defeat to Brandon Figueroa in front of his home crowd in Liverpool on Saturday.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal's 9-point lead means 'nothing'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has claimed Arsenal's nine-point lead at the top of the Premier League means "nothing" despite his team piling the pressure on Manchester City.

items.title: Carrick: Utd haven't 'cracked it' after Spurs win
items.summary: Michael Carrick is not being fooled into thinking Manchester United have "cracked it" after his dream start continued by beating a Tottenham side that captain Cristian Romero apologised to after his costly red card.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: Wrexham hero Mullin hits out at club after exit
items.summary: Paul Mullin has hit out at parent club Wrexham, saying he's "never going to accept" the way he was frozen out of the team over the past 18 months.

items.title: Droomdebuut voor Terho: 'Mijn vader zegt altijd dat ik niet kan koppen'
items.summary: Sparta-debutant Casper Terho zal de wedstrijd van zaterdagavond niet snel vergeten. Niet alleen deed de Fin voor het eerst mee in een wedstrijd van de Rotterdammers, hij redde ook nog eens eigenhandig een punt voor zijn nieuwe ploeg. Vanzelfsprekend was de rechtsbuiten daar erg blij mee.

items.title: European football: Jacquet sustains ‘serious’ injury days after agreeing Liverpool deal
items.summary: 20-year-old Rennes defender fell awkwardly on shoulder Barcelona outclass Mallorca; Hojlund stars for Napoli Liverpool’s new summer signing Jeremy Jacquet has sustained a “serious” shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye. The 20-year-old fell awkwardly in the second half of a 3-1 defeat to Lens in Ligue 1 and appeared in agony as he left the pitch. “For Jeremy, it’s his shoulder, and for Abdelhamid [Ait Boudlal, the other Rennes player injured] it’s muscular,” Beye said in his post-match interview. “We’ll have time to see, but it’s definitely quite serious for both of them.” Continue reading...

items.title: Veldman laakt spel van Heerenveen: 'Dit gedrag is onacceptabel'
items.summary: Robin Veldman staat al bijna een jaar aan het roer bij SC Heerenveen, maar zo'n slechte wedstrijd als die van zaterdagavond tegen FC Twente zag hij nog niet eerder. De trainer was na afloop zeer kritisch over 'het gedrag' van zijn ploeg en stelde dat dit nooit meer mag gebeuren.

items.title: Fortuna en Sparta houden punt over aan knotsgekke debutantenslotfase
items.summary: Sparta heeft zaterdagavond in de laatste seconde een punt gered op bezoek bij Fortuna Sittard. Debutant Yassin Oukili dacht de Limburgers naar de zege te hebben geschoten, maar Sparta-debutant Casper Terho stak daar een stokje voor: 2-2.

items.title: Frustrerend seizoen Fiorentina krijgt nieuw hoofdstuk
items.summary: Zaterdag stonden er vier Eredivisie-wedstrijden op het programma, maar ook in het buitenland werd gevoetbald. Onder meer Manchester United Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund en Napoli kwamen in actie.

items.title: Lyon verlengt indrukwekkende zegereeks ondanks rode kaart Endrick
items.summary: Olympique Lyon heeft zaterdagavond ten koste van FC Nantes voor de zesde keer op rij gewonnen in de Ligue 1. De ploeg van Ruben Kluivert en Hans Hateboer eindigde de wedstrijd met tien man door een directe rode kaart voor Real Madrid-huurling Endrick.

items.title: Eddie Howe facing ‘harsh reality’ after Newcastle’s home loss to Brentford
items.summary: ‘I think I’ve got to do better, I’ve got to do more’ Ouattara’s late goal gave Brentford 3-2 victory Eddie Howe said he was facing a “harsh reality” and felt “angry” with himself after watching his Newcastle team lose 3-2 at home to Brentford on Saturday evening. It was their fourth defeat in five games in all competitions and left the Saudi Arabian-owned club 12th in the Premier League. A soundtrack of boos greeted the final whistle but Newcastle’s manager did not complain. Continue reading...

items.title: England cement status as France’s chief Six Nations rivals — but Borthwick demands more
items.summary: If France set the benchmark with their emphatic opening‑night victory over Ireland, then England proved they are the main rivals to their Six Nations crown.

items.title: Why Super Bowl 60 is the most unlikely in NFL history
items.summary: Welcome to the most unlikely Super Bowl in history. At least, that's what the odds tell you.

items.title: Celtic require extra time to book Scottish Cup QF spot
items.summary: Celtic needed extra time to advance to the quarter-finals of the Scottish Cup after a dramatic 2-1 come-from-behind victory over Dundee in front of a half-empty Celtic Park as St Mirren, Dunfermline and Partick Thistle also progressed to the last eight.

items.title: Honest Howe: I'm not doing my job well enough at Newcastle
items.summary: Eddie Howe has admitted he is "not doing my job well enough at the moment" as the pressure on the Newcastle head coach mounted after a 3-2 defeat to Brentford.

items.title: Gyökeres’ gifts of bundling and poaching suggest Arsenal have found the real thing | Barney Ronay
items.summary: After a slow start, the Sweden striker is now appearing regularly on the scoresheet with six goals in eight games At times during that difficult start to his first season at Arsenal Viktor Gyökeres looked more likely to fall over than score a Premier League goal. But why compromise? Why choose one over the other? Against Sunderland Gyökeres found a third way. He fell over while scoring. Maybe you can have it all. It made for a deeply wholesome moment. Gyökeres couldn’t help smiling ruefully behind his peekaboo celebration, even as he was mobbed fondly by his teammates. The goal was also his first touch seven minutes after coming on, a goal to kill a game Arsenal had eased through in low gear, and which always felt like a matter of housekeeping, a question of exactly how and how many, from the moment they took the lead just before half-time. Continue reading...

items.title: Ouattara scores late to give Brentford win over Newcastle
items.summary: Dango Ouattara scores in the 85th minute to give Brentford a 3-2 win over Newcastle United, as the visitors win their first game at St James' Park since 1934.

items.title: Gyokeres scores twice as Arsenal beat Sunderland
items.summary: Viktor Gyokeres scores two goals as Arsenal comfortably beat Sunderland 3-0 to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table to nine points.
